title: Kings of Leon — Aug 2021 listening list
html:
TL;DR

  • Apple Note from Aug 15, 2021 (Yahir age 18, two weeks pre-Peru) — bare four-band listening list. Title: "Kings of Leon". Body: "Hippocampus / Radio Head / Black Sunday by Cyprus Hill" (Cypress Hill misspelled).
  • Pre-OSU, pre-Peru, this is Yahir building a travel soundtrack — the four acts span Southern rock (Kings of Leon), indie-pop (Hippo Campus), art-rock (Radiohead), and West Coast hip-hop (Cypress Hill). No through-line — pure what someone told me to check out list, the music-version of the Ranch grocery vocab note from a year earlier.
  • Sits in the listening-cluster gap between the 2020 grants-pass-teen-era notes (which cite no music) and the 2022 jazz pillars discovery in 2022 10 25 ahmad jamal stream — making this the only specifically-named pop/rock listening note in the corpus's first half. The jazz era will overwrite this taste, mostly.
  • Black Sunday (1993) is the second Cypress Hill album — Yahir is fishing in his older-brother / dad's likely catalog, not Spotify's then-current Discover Weekly. Suggests the recommendation came from a person, not an algorithm.
  • The misspellings (Radio Head, Cyprus Hill) are characteristic voice-to-text or fast-typed Yahir — a sensorium snapshot, not a curated playlist. Worth scanning for any Spotify/Apple Music export from this era to triangulate.

Key fragments

"Hippocampus / Radio Head / Black Sunday by Cyprus Hill"

Why it matters in the graph

  • Only granular pop/rock listening data in the 2018–2022 stretch — fills a gap in the music graph where everything else is jazz or absent.
  • Precedes the 2022 jazz turn by ~14 months — useful for what music did Yahir listen to before he was a jazz person lookups.

Stub note

  • The four bands (Kings of Leon, Hippo Campus, Radiohead, Cypress Hill) are queued to the log Potential Entities list rather than stubbed — single-mention, no signal of recurrence yet. Promote on second mention.

Privacy

  • No PII.
